stories.title: View all connected devices
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to see a list of all my registered devices so that I know which ones are currently online.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given I am logged into the web dashboard
When I navigate to the Devices page
Then I see a list of all registered Devices
And each Device entry shows: hostname, HardwareID (truncated), DeviceStatus (ONLINE or OFFLINE), and last_seen timestamp

Given a Device's last_seen timestamp is less than 60 seconds ago
When the Devices list is rendered
Then that Device's DeviceStatus is displayed as ONLINE

Given a Device's last_seen timestamp is 60 seconds or more ago
When the Devices list is rendered
Then that Device's DeviceStatus is displayed as OFFLINE

stories.title: Take a screenshot of a device
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to take a screenshot of a specific device so that I can see what is currently on its screen.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given a Device with DeviceStatus ONLINE is selected on the dashboard
When I click 'Take Screenshot'
Then a Command of CommandType CMD_SCREENSHOT is created with CommandStatus PENDING
And the Command is associated with the selected Device

Given a CMD_SCREENSHOT Command has been dispatched to an ONLINE Device
When the Device's client executes the command
Then a CommandResult with status SUCCESS is returned
And the CommandResult output contains a base64-encoded PNG image

Given a Device with DeviceStatus OFFLINE is selected
When I click 'Take Screenshot'
Then a CMD_SCREENSHOT Command is created with status PENDING
And it enters the CommandQueue for that Device

stories.title: Schedule a shutdown in 1 hour
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to schedule a shutdown for my PC in 1 hour so that I don't leave it running all night.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given I am on the Device detail page for 'Gaming PC'
When I select 'Power Options' → 'Schedule Shutdown' and enter 3600 seconds
Then a Command of CommandType CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN is created
And the CommandPayload contains {seconds: 3600}
And the CommandStatus is PENDING

Given a CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN Command with payload {seconds: 3600} is received by the client
When the client executes the command
Then the OS shutdown timer is set for 3600 seconds
And a CommandResult with status SUCCESS is returned

Given I attempt to create a CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN Command with payload {seconds: -10}
When the server validates the payload
Then the Command is rejected
And an error is returned: 'seconds must be a positive integer'

stories.title: Cancel a scheduled shutdown
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to cancel a scheduled shutdown if I change my mind.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given a CMD_SCHEDULED_SHUTDOWN Command exists with CommandStatus QUEUED or RUNNING
When I click 'Cancel' on that Command in the dashboard
Then a CancelCommand action is issued
And the CommandStatus transitions to CANCELLED

Given a Command with CommandStatus SUCCESS
When I attempt to cancel it
Then the cancellation is rejected
And an error is returned: 'Cannot cancel a Command in terminal state SUCCESS'

stories.title: Set device volume to 50%
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to set the volume on my PC to 50% remotely so I can adjust music without getting up.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given I am on the Device detail page
When I use the Volume slider to select 50
Then a Command of CommandType CMD_SET_VOLUME is created
And the CommandPayload is {level: 50, mute: false}

Given a CMD_SET_VOLUME Command with payload {level: 50, mute: false} is received by the client
When the client executes the command
Then the system audio volume is set to 50%
And a CommandResult with status SUCCESS and output 'Volume set to 50%' is returned

Given I attempt to create a CMD_SET_VOLUME Command with payload {level: 150}
When the server validates the payload
Then the Command is rejected
And an error is returned: 'level must be in range 0–100'

stories.title: Save a URL as a Preset
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to save a YouTube link as a Preset so I can easily reopen it on my PC later.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given I am on the Presets page
When I enter name='LoFi Playlist' and url='https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=abc123' and click Save
Then a Preset is created with the given name and URL
And it appears in the Presets list

Given a Preset named 'LoFi Playlist' exists
When I click 'Open on Gaming PC'
Then a Command of CommandType CMD_OPEN_PRESET is issued to 'Gaming PC'
And the CommandPayload contains {url: 'https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=abc123'}

Given I attempt to create a Preset with an empty name
When I click Save
Then the Preset is rejected
And an error is returned: 'PresetName must not be empty'

stories.title: See real-time CPU and RAM usage
stories.narrative: As a user, I want to see real-time CPU and RAM usage of my PC so I can detect performance issues.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given my Device is ONLINE
When the dashboard renders the Device detail page
Then I see SystemMetrics showing cpu_percent and ram_percent
And these values were collected within the last 30 seconds

Given a CMD_SYSTEM_INFO Command is executed by the client
When the execution completes
Then a CommandResult is returned with output containing cpu_percent (float 0–100) and ram_percent (float 0–100)

stories.title: Smart suggestion: Close Browser when RAM > 90%
stories.narrative: As a user, I want the system to suggest 'Close Browser' when my PC's RAM usage exceeds 90%, so I can act before a crash.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given SystemMetrics for a Device shows ram_percent = 92.0
When the SmartSuggestionEngine evaluates the MetricsThreshold (threshold: 90)
Then a ThresholdExceeded event is emitted
And a suggestion 'Close Browser' is displayed in the dashboard for that Device

stories.title: Queue commands for offline devices
stories.narrative: As a user, I want commands to be queued if my device is offline and executed automatically when it comes back online.

acceptance_criteria.gherkin:
Given a Device has DeviceStatus OFFLINE
When I issue a Command to that Device
Then the Command is created with CommandStatus PENDING
And it is stored in the CommandQueue for that Device

Given a Device with 3 PENDING Commands in its CommandQueue comes back ONLINE
When the Device's client polls GET /api/v1/commands/pending
Then all 3 Commands are returned in FIFO order
And each Command's status transitions to SENT as it is dispatched
